name: Glitch Post
description: Reproducible twin of create_glitch: a noise driver displaces the source via a Displace TOP, then a GLSL TOP adds chromatic RGB split and horizontal band tearing. A synthetic animated noiseTOP stands in as the source so it previews offline; swap the source for a selectTOP to glitch real footage. After import, bind the driver's tz to absTime.seconds * <speed> for moving glitches (manual-wire — RecipeSchema parameters take constants only). Offline-validated against RecipeSchema; UNVERIFIED pending live cook-check.

nodes.name: source
nodes.comment: Synthetic source — swap for a selectTOP / moviefileinTOP to glitch real content.

nodes.name: driver
nodes.comment: Low-res displacement driver; block_size controls the tear chunkiness.

nodes.name: displace
nodes.comment: Displaces the source horizontally by the driver — the structural glitch.

nodes.name: rgbshift
nodes.comment: Chromatic RGB split + per-band horizontal tearing.

glsl_uniforms.name: uShift
glsl_uniforms.label: RGB Shift
glsl_uniforms.description: Horizontal separation of the R/B channels.

glsl_uniforms.name: uAmount
glsl_uniforms.label: Amount
glsl_uniforms.description: Overall glitch intensity (scales both tearing and RGB split).

glsl_uniforms.name: uTime
glsl_uniforms.label: Time
glsl_uniforms.description: Tearing animation phase. After import set to absTime.seconds for moving glitches (manual-wire — RecipeSchema parameters take constants only).

preview_description: A colorful noise field shredded with sideways band-tears and a cyan/red chromatic fringe — a clean datamosh-style glitch look.
